page: Microsoft Azure EC16as_v5 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the ECASv5 server family. Powered by AMD processors and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 16 dedicated virtual CPUs and 128.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, positioning the server within the memory-optimized category.

The instance does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met using external volumes. It is Premium Storage capable, allowing integration with high-performance persistent disks. There are zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with this instance. No graphics processing units are present, making it unsuitable for hardware-accelerated rendering or machine learning training workloads.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the EC16as_v5 offers a high memory density per dedicated vCPU, which is advantageous for workloads that require large in-memory datasets without needing a proportional increase in compute cores. This resource balance is designed for memory-intensive applications such as relational databases, in-memory caches, data analytics pipelines, and enterprise applications that demand significant RAM capacity.
description: Microsoft Azure EC16as_v5 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine from the ECASv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD processors and 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The instance does not include local storage but is Premium Storage capable, allowing connection to external high-performance storage volumes. It contains zero GPUs and zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This memory-optimized configuration is designed for workloads requiring substantial RAM capacity relative to compute resources, such as relational databases, in-memory caching, and data analytics. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance, while the high memory density offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure EC16as_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated AMD vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and Premium Storage compatibility without local disk storage.
